EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Persistent areas of freezing fog with the visibility reduced to near zero at times resulted in a collision between two cars.

There was one fatality and one critical injury.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 9-year-old Reno girl died on 19 July from injuries she suffered when strong winds blew an inflatable bounce house with three children inside into power lines.  The Washoe County Sheriff's Office says the bounce house was uplifted about 10 feet and got caught on the lines in a south Reno neighborhood. Deputies and firefighters rescued the children. They treated two for minor injuries at the scene and transported the 9-year-old girl to the hospital, where she succumbed to her injuries 19 July. The incident occurred on the afternoon of 14 July around 1500PST. Authorities told KOLO-TV the bounce house seemed to be secured to the ground but was caught by an especially strong gust of wind. Winds were sustained 15-20 mph with gusts 20-25 mph at nearby Reno-Tahoe International Airport around the incident time.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moisture from the eastern Pacific Ocean and the Gulf of California moved northward around the periphery of high pressure centered over the Four-Corners region of the Rockies the 24th into the 25th. This moisture, coupled with the weak flow and increased instability in the lower to middle portions of the atmosphere, resulted in storms producing heavy rains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: US Forest Service employee reported minor street flooding on the corner of Glendale Avenue and Meredith Way in Sparks due to heavy rain from a thunderstorm.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Washoe County Official reported that runoff from thunderstorms caused flash flooding near Swan Lake. A nearby RAWS approximately 3 miles to the east of the flooded area reported 1.53 inches of rainfall in a 3-hour period from 25 July 1640PST to 1940PST. No damage or cleanup information was available.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: KTVN Channel 2 News reported that the roof on Reed High School leaked during heavy rainfall from thunderstorms on the afternoon of 25 July. A nearby mesonet reported 0.21 inches of rainfall from 1703PST to 1803PST. According to Washoe County School District, three sections of the roof were being replaced at the time. All three sections leaked in multiple areas. Walls, ceilings, floors, and 36 computers were damaged by the rain water. Preliminary damage estimates were $40-60K according to the school district.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Warm temperatures, along with dry conditions, occurred the 23rd through the end of the month. Breezy conditions occurred on the 24th and 25th, but combined effects of winds and humidity did not reach critical fire weather criteria.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Long Valley Fire started near the Red Rock exit along US Highway 395 and spread across the California/Nevada border into northwestern Nevada the 24th before being contained the 27th. The fire burned 2438 acres, and one structure was destroyed. The Federal Emergency Management Agency stated that the fire threatened 250-300 homes in and around Rancho Haven (Nevada). The fire also threatened five buildings, infrastructure, utilities, equipment, farms, ranches, and a watershed in the area. Mandatory evacuations were ordered for approximately 800 people before containment was reached. Firefighting costs were approximately $600K, according to the Nevada Bureau of Land Management.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A melting snowpack combined with periods of rain to produce increased runoff into area creeks and rivers.  Given how slow rises are in northern Nevada, some of the floods last for weeks.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water from rain showers combined with an above normal snowpack to produce runoff into Salmon Falls Creek.  The creek rose in height to approximately 11 feet at the USGS gauge, causing the parking area at the rest stop to close.  Water eventually receded from the rest stop and it was reopened.

Liquid totals were between 1 and 2 inches across foothill locations west of Reno, Carson City, and Minden. For the Reno-Carson City-Minden areas, liquid totals ranged between 0.50 and 1.00 inches. Snowfall totals along the higher terrain of the Carson Range were between 1 to 2 feet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mesonet station EW1713 3 miles east-northeast of Verdi reported 1.08 inches of rainfall in a 48-hour period from 6 December 2015PST to 8 December 2015PST at an elevation of 5807 feet MSL.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Eagle Wash Road (a dirt road) was obliterated by flooding and will have to be resurfaced. Nelson Landing Turnaround will also have to be repaired.

CZ_NAME: LAKE MEAD/LAKE MOHAVE NATIONAL RECREATION AREA
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: North winds behind a strong cold front did minor damage in southern Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds tore a panel from the Riverside Resort in Laughlin.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated thunderstorms formed across parts of west-central Nevada, moving quickly northward into northern Nevada. The strongest storms produced wind gusts between 45 and 50 mph and blowing dust across parts of the basin and range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong outflow winds from nearby decaying thunderstorms blew a small tree down, blew the siding of a single-wide trailer off, blew metal roofing panels off another single-wide trailer, and damaged a metal overhang on a flat-roofed store. Wind gusts were estimated to be 50-55 mph. The damage was estimated based upon the costs of the materials damaged.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A longwave trough moved across the northwestern US the 13th through the 17th. This trough brought breezy conditions each afternoon to western Nevada and eastern California.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Jasper Fire in northern Sun Valley burned 1,165 acres from 13 July 1400PST to 16 July 1700PST. Sierra Front Dispatch Center stated that one home and two outbuildings were destroyed. At one point more than 100 homes were threatened, and the Washoe County Sheriff���s Office was going door-to-door urging people to evacuate. Firefighting costs were approximately $769K USD according to the Nevada Bureau of Land Management.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A trough of low pressure along the West Coast moved inland late on the 21st bringing gusty winds through the 22nd and high elevation snowfall to Western Nevada the 22nd through 23rd. While not a huge snowmaker, this storm hit at a busy travel time.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mesonet atop Slide Mountain (elevation 9650 feet MSL) recorded a wind gust of 77 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A trough of low pressure dropped into western Nevada from the west-northwest on the 13th and produced rain and high elevation snow across the Lake Tahoe Basin starting the morning of the 13th. Locations in the Lake Tahoe Basin received 0.25 inches to 1.50 inches of liquid. Higher elevations within the Tahoe Basin, mainly above 8000 feet, reported snow totals ranging from 2 to 15 inches. Only trace amounts of rain were reported across western Nevada and into the Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mount Rose Ski Area reported 11 inches of snowfall at their base elevation of 8260 feet MSL in a 48-hour period from early on the 13th through the morning of the 15th.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two-day snowfall amounts ranged from a trace to 4 inches across much of the Greater Reno-Carson City-Minden Area, with higher totals closer to the Carson Range.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong northerly winds behind a cold front were locally enhanced in the Colorado River Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A large tree was blown down at the Laughlin Fire Station.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front brought strong northerly winds to the Mojave Desert.  Isolated high winds occurred in the Las Vegas Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The peak gust occurred at Nellis Air Force Base. A large tree was blown down at Sahara and Durango.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Big Bend Fire burned 225 acres south of Laughlin and destroyed a few power poles along Needles Highway.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wildfire at Big Bend State Park burned 225 acres and destroyed some power poles.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Excessive heat conditions helped maintain thunderstorms which rolled in from the east late in the evening, bringing damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The peak gust occurred at 2313 PST. Several houseboats broke loose from their anchors and were pushed onshore, and a floating bathroom was pushed into a dock.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm with very impressive supercellular structure and radar presentation (unusual for the region) produced large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A driver on Highway 95 reported golf ball size hail.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong southwesterly winds affected much of southern Nevada. One station reported a high wind gust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred at Mercury-Desert Rock (KDRA).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lingering heat fueled one last round of isolated thunderstorms, one of which produced high winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One boat was swamped, and another was blown into the shore.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: On the 16th, a strong cold front brought a sharp drop in temperature along with strong winds to portions of Pershing, Churchill, and Mineral counties.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nevada DOT reported a fatality from a single-vehicle accident on US Highway 95 at mile marker 76 near Schurz on the afternoon of the 6th at approximately 1530 to 1630PST. Nevada DOT called NWS Reno around 1545PST, indicating that an overturned semi-truck had closed the highway. The Nevada DOT mesonet at Schurz reported a peak gust of 50 mph from the west at approximately 1335PST. Numerous gusts above 40 mph started at 1245PST and lasted through 1545PST.
